pi_items.verified_value: Natural Earth 1:110m Admin 0 Countries GeoJSON dataset available at https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvkelso/natural-earth-vector/master/geojson/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.geojson (838,726 bytes)
pi_items.source: Downloaded and verified via wget; dataset confirmed as official Natural Earth public domain data
Extracted from GeoJSON geometry of Somalia feature in ne_110m_admin_0_countries.geojson by analyzing all polygon coordinates

Calculated using Shapely's polylabel algorithm (tolerance=0.01) to find pole of inaccessibility at (43.012006, 2.486679), then computed geodetic distance to nearest boundary point using proper lat/lon to km conversion (111.32 km/degree for latitude, 111.22 km/degree for longitude at Somalia's latitude of ~2.5°)
Same calculation as min_distance_km; the pole of inaccessibility distance is approximately 198.18 km, which is well below the claimed maximum

pi_items.verified_value: EPSG:3857 is the official Web Mercator projection used by Google Maps, OpenStreetMap, and Bing Maps. Covers area between 85.06°S and 85.06°N
pi_items.source: EPSG.io official register and PROJ documentation confirm EPSG:3857 specifications

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real, public-domain Natural Earth dataset (ne_110m_admin_0_countries.geojson from GitHub, version with 177 countries). Somalia's actual geographic bounding box in WGS84 is [40.98105, -1.68325, 51.13387, 12.02464], with the maximum latitude slightly exceeding the metadata claim of 12.0 (actual: 12.02464). Using Shapely's polylabel algorithm, Somalia's pole of inaccessibility is located at approximately (43.012, 2.487) with a distance to the nearest boundary of approximately 198.18 km. This actual distance is slightly less than the metadata's claimed minimum of 200 km (difference of 1.82 km, or ~1.5% error). The claimed maximum of 600 km is verified as correct.
